title: Remediate slop defects

tasks.title: Add spec requirements for the two corrected correctness invariants (AC8)
tasks.verification: Added rq-worktreeLeaseLiveness01 to .adv/specs/worktree-lifecycle/spec.json (v1.5.0→1.6.0, 14→15 reqs) + doc mirror; added rq-agendaDurableParse01 to .adv/specs/advance-meta/spec.json (v1.18.0→1.19.0, 46→47 reqs) + doc mirror. Both JSON files validated round-trip. Each rq has 2 Given/When/Then scenarios. evidence_policy=review (docs/spec task, not_applicable TDD).

tasks.title: QUAL-002: shared process-liveness helper + converge 3 implementations (AC1, SC1)
tasks.verification: QUAL-002 fixed. New utils/process-liveness.ts (isProcessAlive: ESRCH→dead, EPERM/other→alive). Converged 3 sites: worktree-lease.ts uses it at reclaim path; session/index.ts re-exports as isPidAlive; worker-lock.ts isLockOwnerAlive delegates. RED: process-liveness.test.ts failed (module missing, runId tr_mqtmj9zx exit1). GREEN: 36 tests pass incl new EPERM-no-reclaim AC1 test (runId tr_mqtmm1vw exit0). Workflow-bundle boundary intact (4/4, runId tr_mqtmmdx8). Respects DONT2/C2/C3/C5; verifies AC1 + rq-worktreeLeaseLiveness01 conformance (AC8).

tasks.title: QUAL-003: agenda durability — log malformed lines + guard compaction (AC2, SC2)
tasks.verification: QUAL-003 fixed. agenda.ts: parseLine now returns a tagged ParseLineResult distinguishing skip/malformed/meta/item; loadAgenda logs malformed lines via appendDebugLog("agenda",…), counts them, returns skippedMalformed (additive field, all callers destructure subsets); compactAgenda skips rewrite + warns when skippedMalformed>0 so malformed content is never destroyed. RED: 3 failed incl malformed-line-destroyed (runId tr_mqtmq5vj exit1). GREEN: 4/4 pass (runId tr_mqtmro5b exit0). Respects DONT3/C3/C5; verifies AC2 + rq-agendaDurableParse01 conformance (AC8).

tasks.title: QUAL-005: mesh-issue parse-failure signal (AC4, SC3)
tasks.verification: QUAL-005 fixed. Added parseFailed?:boolean to MeshIssueResult + ListMeshIssuesResult + getGhIssue return; set parseFailed:true in all 3 JSON.parse catches (createMeshIssue/listMeshIssues/getGhIssue). archive-mesh.ts consumer now treats result.parseFailed as an error instead of silent exit-0 success. RED: 2 failed (runId tr_mqtmuric exit1). GREEN: 24/24 pass (runId tr_mqtmw722 exit0). Respects DONT3/C5; verifies AC4.

tasks.title: STRUCT-002: centralize completed-workflow recovery glue (AC5, SC4)
tasks.verification: STRUCT-002 fixed. Added single helper recoverReleaseGateIfWorkflowCompleted(error, ctx) in change.ts; replaced the 3 byte-identical catch blocks (1619/1649/1666) and their duplicated dynamic imports with one call each. Behavior-preserving. Verify: recovery-probe, _recovery-writers, change.archive-phase9, change.status-repair, change.archive-repair, gate suites — 97/97 pass (runId tr_mqtmywu3 exit0). Respects C2/C5; verifies AC5.

tasks.title: STRUCT-004: single source of truth for change/workflow prefixes (AC7, SC4)
tasks.verification: STRUCT-004 fixed. Added CHANGE_BRANCH_PREFIX + CHANGE_WORKFLOW_PREFIX to temporal/contracts.ts (SSOT). Removed 3 duplicate const defs (worktree/state.ts, visibility-claim-queries.ts, list-change-workflows.ts) → imports. Converted all campsite raw literals: client.ts buildChangeWorkflowId, activities.ts branch builder, census.ts/triage.ts(x3)/index.ts(x2)/git-finalize.ts(x2) startsWith guards. Verify: workflow-bundle-boundary (4), client (4), visibility-claim-queries (12), list-change-workflows (11), git-finalize (81), census (5), triage (13) + earlier worktree/ index suite all pass (runId tr_mqtndvqf + tr_mqtnb1g8 exit0). Respects C2/C5; verifies AC7.

tasks.title: QUAL-001 disposition: docstring cite (AC9, SC5)
tasks.verification: QUAL-001 dispositioned as accepted residual risk. Added a docstring to classifyDestructiveBash in trunk-write-firewall.ts citing the advance-meta spec (rq-twf01.7) and ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md:467 residual-risk note (shell indirection / script-internal writes are accepted, not a defect). No firewall behavior change. Verify: trunk-write-firewall.test + regression.test pass (runId tr_mqtnf7te exit0). Respects DONT1/DONT4; verifies AC9.

tasks.title: STRUCT-001 disposition: record deferral to ROADMAP #82 (AC10, SC5)
tasks.verification: STRUCT-001 dispositioned: deferred to ROADMAP #82, no change.ts decomposition (DONT5 respected). Confirmed ROADMAP.md:54 #82 "Reduce ESLint complexity violations across plugin/src" actively tracks the plugin/src complexity reduction that covers the change.ts god-object. No code change in this task; deferral will be recorded in the executive summary at acceptance. evidence_policy=artifact_reference; verifies AC10.

tasks.title: STRUCT-003: shared gate-completion polling helper (AC6, SC4)
tasks.verification: STRUCT-003 fixed. Added shared waitForGateCompletion(handle, gateId, opts) to _adapters.ts (single SSOT, exported constants 60/500). gate.ts waitForGateCompletionResult and change.ts waitForArchiveReleaseGateCompletion now delegate to it; removed both local poll loops + delay wrappers + duplicate constants. Added 3 direct unit tests for the shared helper + updated gate.test mock factory to mirror it. Verify: gate.test (28), change.archive-phase9 (27), _adapters (30 incl new) all pass (runId tr_mqtn5liz + tr_mqtn6sni, exit0). Respects C2; verifies AC6.

tasks.title: QUAL-004: observable archive-cleanup warnings (AC3, SC3)
tasks.verification: QUAL-004 fixed. Replaced the 3 empty `// warning-only` catches in change.ts archive-cleanup block (removeChangeDir, advWorktreeCleanup, deleteChangeBranch) with contextual logger.warn calls including change id + op + error text. Confirmed the other 2 cleanup catches (4596/4598 region) already push to archiveResult.errors (not defects). Verify: change.archive-phase9 (27) + change.archive-repair (11) pass (runId tr_mqtn8rbr exit0). evidence_policy=review (observability change). Respects DONT3.

tasks.title: Final verification + touched-scope follow-up scan (AC11, SC5)
tasks.verification: Final verification complete (AC11, SC5). `pnpm run check` PASS (schemas:check, typecheck, test-isolation, lockfile, lint, format:check — runId tr_mqtnqoyr exit0). Full `pnpm test`: 277 files / 3933 tests pass; 47 failures triaged — 5 (gate.release-enforcement) were caused by the STRUCT-003 _adapters mock boundary and are now FIXED (added waitForGateCompletion to that test's mock; 38/38 gate tests pass runId tr_mqtnp899). Remaining 42 are pre-existing on trunk, proven non-causal via `git diff --name-only 2b5dbe40 HEAD` (none of the failing subsystems touched): 32 STSL/service 5s timeouts (need live Temporal, absent in env), 5 observability AdvLifecycleState SA mismatch (contracts.ts diff is only 2 string consts; AdvLifecycleState not added by me), 3 stale spec-version assertions (advance-workflow 1.22.0 / backlog-coordination 1.4.0 already on trunk), 1 deploy-local prompt length, 1 unrelated rq-changeLifecycleState01 citation. Touched-scope scan: 1 CHANGE_WORKFLOW_PREFIX def (contracts.ts), 0 empty archive-cleanup catches, agenda logs malformed lines, lease uses shared isProcessAlive. Every finding fixed or dispositioned; none silently dropped. Respects C1.

subagent_reports.topic: Design validation for remediateSlopDefects (KD1-KD9 + 2 spec laws)

sources.label: worktree-lease.ts isPidAlive (buggy)
sources.summary: Current isPidAlive has bare catch{return false} — EPERM wrongly classified dead, enabling reclaim of a live peer. KD1 target.

sources.label: session/index.ts isPidAlive (already correct)
sources.summary: Existing exported isPidAlive ALREADY implements the KD1 target semantics: ESRCH->false, EPERM->true, other->true. Re-export from new helper preserves session/index.test.ts:18 import; logic is duplicated, not novel.

sources.label: worker-lock.ts injectable seam
sources.summary: isLockOwnerAlive(pid, isPidAlive?) already injectable; default branch uses code !== 'ESRCH' (EPERM-tolerant). KD1 delegating default to helper preserves seam + tests (worker-lock.test.ts:90/117/140).

sources.label: agenda.ts silent parse + compaction destroy path
sources.summary: parseLine catch{return null} silently drops malformed lines; loadAgenda skips them; compactAgenda rewrites file from parseable items only -> malformed lines permanently destroyed. KD2 data-loss claim is REAL; block-compaction-when-skipped correctly prevents it.

sources.label: project-wisdom.ts mirror
sources.summary: Mirror logs malformed JSON via appendDebugLog('wisdom',...) but has NO compaction destroy-path, so the agenda case legitimately needs the EXTRA compaction-skip guard beyond the logging mirror.

sources.label: advance-meta spec residual-risk text
sources.summary: rq-twf01 body states 'Shell indirection and script-internal writes are accepted residual risk documented in ADV instructions'; scenario explicitly says documented in ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md. KD8 docstring claim is accurate but spec points doc locus at ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md, not the source docstring.

sources.label: workflow-bundle reachability of contracts.ts
sources.summary: contracts.ts IS workflow-bundle-reachable. Boundary test bans node:*/storage/tools imports and defineUpdate only. KD7 adds string-only consts with no new imports -> boundary-safe (existing consts at contracts.ts:14-18 set precedent).

sources.label: existing duplicate prefix defs
sources.summary: CHANGE_WORKFLOW_PREFIX='adv/change/' duplicated (2 const defs + client.ts template literal). KD7 SSOT consolidation confirmed; design's 3rd site (worktree/state.ts:220) path appears stale — no storage/worktree/state.ts found; verify exact file before edit.

sources.label: worktree-lifecycle spec requirements (no collision)
sources.summary: No rq-worktreeLeaseLiveness01 present; ID does not collide. Lease-liveness invariant fits the cleanup/safety theme of the capability.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is sound, behavior-preserving, and boring/proven across all four dimensions. CORRECTNESS: EPERM->alive is the correct SAFE direction for lease reclaim (avoids reclaiming a live but non-signalable peer process owned by another uid; false-alive is fail-safe vs false-dead which is data-unsafe). The target semantics already exist verbatim in session/index.ts:47-57, so KD1 is consolidation of a proven implementation, not invention. KD7 does NOT risk the workflow-bundle import boundary: contracts.ts is reachable from workflows.ts but the boundary walker only forbids node:*/storage/tools imports and defineUpdate; two plain string consts add no imports and match existing precedent. KD2's data-loss path is genuine (parseLine swallow -> compaction rewrite destroys malformed lines) and the block-compaction-when-skipped guard closes it without introducing a new silent-failure (DONT3 respected) — it warns + skips. SIMPLICITY: no materially simpler approach; each item is the minimal in-place fix. One simplification note: KD1 could re-export session/index.ts's isPidAlive directly rather than creating a third process-liveness.ts module, but the design's dedicated helper centralizes 3 call sites (lease, session, worker-lock) which justifies the module. SPEC-LAW: residual-risk claim is REAL and verbatim in spec; KD8 docstring is additive and accurate, though spec designates ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md as the canonical doc locus — recommend KD8 docstring CITE that file rather than restate. New rq IDs do not collide. SEAMS: KD1 re-export + worker-lock injectable seam both verified intact against existing tests.

validation.notes: VALIDATED with two minor cautions, no conflicts. (C1) KD1 partially duplicates already-correct session/index.ts:40-57 logic — ensure the new helper and session re-export converge on ONE implementation to avoid a third divergent copy; tests session/index.test.ts:18 + worker-lock.test.ts must stay green. (C2) KD7 3rd duplicate-prefix site cited as worktree/state.ts:219-220 could not be located at that path (no plugin/src/storage/worktree/state.ts); confirm the exact file/line for the CHANGE_BRANCH_PREFIX + 3rd CHANGE_WORKFLOW_PREFIX def before editing, else the SSOT consolidation (AC7) is incomplete. (C3) KD8: spec scenario rq-twf01.7 names ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md as the documentation locus — KD8 docstring should reference that file to stay spec-consistent (AC9). No data-loss, no boundary, no security/release blockers found.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Proceed to planning. The design is VALIDATED with simplicity bias satisfied — every workstream is a boring in-place fix with behavior preservation. Before execution: (1) resolve the KD7 3rd-prefix-site path discrepancy (worktree/state.ts:219-220 not found at that path) so AC7 SSOT consolidation is complete and not partial; (2) make KD1's process-liveness.ts the single canonical implementation that session/index.ts re-exports (it already has the exact correct semantics) to avoid a third divergent isPidAlive; (3) point the KD8 classifyDestructiveBash docstring at ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md per rq-twf01.7 rather than restating the residual-risk prose. The EPERM->alive direction (AC1) is confirmed the correct fail-safe choice; KD2 compaction-skip (AC2) genuinely closes a real data-destruction path with no new silent-failure; KD7 (AC7) is workflow-bundle-boundary-safe. New spec IDs do not collide.
follow_ups: Confirm exact file:line for KD7 3rd CHANGE_WORKFLOW_PREFIX def + CHANGE_BRANCH_PREFIX def (design cites worktree/state.ts:219-220 but no plugin/src/storage/worktree/state.ts exists at that path).
Ensure KD1 converges session/index.ts isPidAlive (already correct) and new process-liveness.ts on one implementation; keep session/index.test.ts:18 + worker-lock.test.ts green.
KD8 docstring should reference ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md per rq-twf01.7 spec scenario, not restate residual-risk prose.

subagent_reports.summary: Inline harden assessment (low-risk internal remediation; one orchestrator synthesis pass, no dedicated scanner fan-out). All release-owned dimensions checked: Test coverage strong (every fix has RED→GREEN or behavior-preserving tests; 2 symmetric parseFailed tests added during harden). AI-slop: change REMOVES slop (tagged unions, typed parseFailed flag, shared liveness/poll/recovery helpers, SSOT prefixes) — no new placeholders/type-erosion. Doc hygiene: spec doc mirrors + docstrings updated, match code, no stale refs or conflicts. Cleanup: no temp/debug/dead artifacts (only os.tmpdir() in tests). Production readiness: improves reliability (lease safety, agenda durability, observable failures); no secrets/perf/complexity regressions; change.ts net-shrank via dedup. Deployment readiness: N/A — no env vars, migrations, external services, CI/CD, or infra changes. Merge compatibility: clean dry-run into origin/trunk. Contract proof: 26/26 rows pass/respected. pnpm run check green (runId tr_mqtq60j6).

findings.summary: Construction-site raw change/${id} literals remain in git-finalize.ts (~13 sites) and change.ts prBranch, alongside the migrated guard/builder sites. AC7 (duplicate definitions removed) is satisfied; this is a non-blocking consistency nit accepted with rationale to avoid expanding the diff across 15+ unrelated sites for zero behavior change.

evidence.label: git-finalize construction literals
evidence.summary: raw change/ construction literals not migrated to CHANGE_BRANCH_PREFIX

follow_ups: Optional future campsite: migrate remaining change/${id} construction literals in git-finalize.ts and change.ts to CHANGE_BRANCH_PREFIX for full SSOT consistency (non-blocking nit).

proposal.approval_evidence: Proposal approved via Tier A command-as-approval: user invoked /adv-discover remediateSlopDefects while proposal checkpoint pending. Problem statement validated against source; Scope (In/Out/Must Not) populated; B/F/S scan clean (no CRITICAL).

discovery.approval_evidence: Criteria approved via Tier A inline reply 'approve'. Agreement persisted; ChangeContract minted (26 items: SC1-5, AC1-11, C1-5, DONT1-5). User decisions: STRUCT-001 deferred to ROADMAP #82, QUAL-001 documented residual risk, spec-law capture for lease-liveness + agenda-durability.

design.approval_evidence: Design persisted (9 KDs, 4 workstreams, 2 DDCs). Leverage scout skipped (bounded prescribed remediation, ~zero opportunity surface). Independent validator (adv-researcher) verdict CAUTION → VALIDATED with 2 minor cautions, no conflicts; all 3 cautions resolved in design (KD7 path confirmed + extra literal sites folded, KD1 SSOT re-export explicit, KD8 cites ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md). No contract-compromise risk, no user-value tradeoff.

planning.approval_evidence: Prep plan approved via Tier A inline reply 'approve'. 11 tasks with full contract traceability (all AC1-11 + SC1-5 covered; all 5 DONT respected). Dependency graph: T1 spec-first → T2/T3; change.ts tasks serialized T6→T7→T4; T11 verify blocked by all. Strict validation passed (NO_DELTAS warning expected — spec edits authored as task T1).

execution.approval_evidence: All 11 tasks done via TDD/verification. 7 fixes (QUAL-002/003/004/005, STRUCT-002/003/004) + 2 spec requirements + 2 dispositions (QUAL-001 residual-risk docstring, STRUCT-001 deferred ROADMAP #82). pnpm run check PASS; full suite green except 42 proven-pre-existing failures (env STSL timeouts + stale version assertions + unrelated subsystems untouched by this change). The only my-caused suite failures (gate.release-enforcement _adapters mock) were fixed. Worktree HEAD fbfa20a8c5e180894b90ad2b59a508bd718622aa.

acceptance.approval_evidence: User accepted via Tier A inline reply 'approve'. Acceptance review verdict APPROVED (0 blockers, 0 issues; 6 minor nits/suggestions deferred to harden) from two independent reviewers. Contract review matrix 26/26 passing/respected, 0 failing. Executive summary persisted + workflow-visible. Preview not_applicable (visual_surface false). pnpm run check green; full suite green except 42 proven-pre-existing failures.

items.text: **SC1** — Multi-session worktree ownership is safe: a live peer's lease is never reclaimed due to an inconclusive liveness probe.
**SC2** — Agenda data is durable: malformed lines are observable and never silently destroyed by compaction.
**SC3** — Archive-cleanup and mesh-issue operational failures are observable, not silently swallowed.
**SC4** — Change-lifecycle code has single sources of truth for recovery glue, gate polling, and change/workflow prefixes.
**SC5** — Every one of the 9 findings has a recorded disposition; none silently dropped.
**AC1** — Lease liveness treats `EPERM`/non-`ESRCH` as alive (dead only on `ESRCH`); a test proves a live peer's lease is not reclaimed on `EPERM`.

**AC2** — Loading agenda JSONL with a malformed line emits an observable log for the dropped line, and compaction does not permanently discard unparsed content without surfacing it; test covers malformed-line load + compaction.
**AC3** — Archive-cleanup failure paths in `change.ts` emit an observable warning (no empty `// warning-only` catch); verification shows a warning when cleanup throws.
**AC4** — A mesh-issue JSON parse failure is distinguishable from a genuine exit-0 success and is treated as a failure by callers; test covers exit-0 + malformed stdout.
**AC5** — Workflow-completed recovery glue in `change.ts` has a single source of truth used by the previously-duplicated catch sites; existing recovery/archive tests pass.
**AC6** — Gate-completion polling has a single shared implementation used by both `gate.ts` and the `change.ts` release-gate path; existing gate/archive tests pass.
**AC7** — `change/` and `adv/change/` prefixes are defined once and imported; duplicate module-local definitions removed; tests pass.
**AC8** — New spec requirements `rq-worktreeLeaseLiveness01` (worktree-lifecycle) and `rq-agendaDurableParse01` (advance-meta) are added with Given/When/Then; implementation conforms.
**AC9** — QUAL-001 disposition recorded as accepted residual risk; `trunk-write-firewall` docstring cites the spec decision; no firewall behavior change.
**AC10** — STRUCT-001 disposition recorded as deferred to ROADMAP #82; no `change.ts` decomposition in this change.
**AC11** — `pnpm run check` passes from `plugin/`; focused tests for all touched modules pass.
**C1** — `pnpm run check` passes from `plugin/`.
**C2** — Preserve enforced layer boundaries (workflow-bundle import boundary; context-snapshot purity).
**C3** — Correctness fixes follow TDD (RED reproduction before fix).
**C4** — No public schema drift without `pnpm run schemas:generate`.
**C5** — Structural-correctness-before-heuristic (P33) preserved.
**DONT1** — Do not weaken the trunk write-protection boundary.
**DONT2** — Do not break the exclusive-worktree-ownership invariant.
**DONT3** — Do not introduce new silent-failure catches while fixing existing ones.
**DONT4** — Do not harden the firewall against shell indirection (spec-classified residual risk).
**DONT5** — Do not decompose `change.ts` (STRUCT-001 deferred to ROADMAP #82).

Agenda durable: agenda.test proves malformed lines logged + compaction preserves malformed content (skipped). runId tr_mqtmro5b.

All 9 findings dispositioned: 7 fixed, QUAL-001 documented residual risk, STRUCT-001 deferred ROADMAP #82. Touched-scope scan clean.

pnpm run check PASS (schemas:check, typecheck, test-isolation, lockfile, lint, format:check). runId tr_mqtnqoyr. Full suite green except 42 proven-pre-existing failures.
pnpm run check passes from plugin/. runId tr_mqtnqoyr.
workflow-bundle-boundary.test.ts green; contracts.ts additions are plain string consts, no new node/storage/tools imports.
TDD red→green for QUAL-002/003/005 (red runIds tr_mqtmj9zx, tr_mqtmq5vj, tr_mqtmuric precede green).
No public schema drift; schemas:check green; spec.json edits are not public Zod schemas.
Structural-correctness preserved: tagged ParseLineResult union (agenda), typed parseFailed flag (mesh), typed liveness helper. No new heuristics own correctness.
trunk-write-firewall classifyDestructiveBash body unchanged (docstring-only diff).
Exclusive-worktree-ownership invariant preserved: EPERM→alive prevents reclaiming a live peer's lease.
No new silent-failure catches: all new/changed catches log or surface (agenda malformed kind, change.ts logger.warn, mesh parseFailed). rg confirms no new bare empty catch.
Firewall not hardened against shell indirection; docstring defers it as accepted residual risk.
change.ts not decomposed (single 5412-line module; only internal helper extraction).

acceptanceCriteria: **AC1** — Lease liveness treats `EPERM`/non-`ESRCH` as alive (dead only on `ESRCH`); a test proves a live peer's lease is not reclaimed on `EPERM`.

**AC2** — Loading agenda JSONL with a malformed line emits an observable log for the dropped line, and compaction does not permanently discard unparsed content without surfacing it; test covers malformed-line load + compaction.
**AC3** — Archive-cleanup failure paths in `change.ts` emit an observable warning (no empty `// warning-only` catch); verification shows a warning when cleanup throws.
**AC4** — A mesh-issue JSON parse failure is distinguishable from a genuine exit-0 success and is treated as a failure by callers; test covers exit-0 + malformed stdout.
**AC5** — Workflow-completed recovery glue in `change.ts` has a single source of truth used by the previously-duplicated catch sites; existing recovery/archive tests pass.
**AC6** — Gate-completion polling has a single shared implementation used by both `gate.ts` and the `change.ts` release-gate path; existing gate/archive tests pass.
**AC7** — `change/` and `adv/change/` prefixes are defined once and imported; duplicate module-local definitions removed; tests pass.
**AC8** — New spec requirements `rq-worktreeLeaseLiveness01` (worktree-lifecycle) and `rq-agendaDurableParse01` (advance-meta) are added with Given/When/Then; implementation conforms.
**AC9** — QUAL-001 disposition recorded as accepted residual risk; `trunk-write-firewall` docstring cites the spec decision; no firewall behavior change.
**AC10** — STRUCT-001 disposition recorded as deferred to ROADMAP #82; no `change.ts` decomposition in this change.
**AC11** — `pnpm run check` passes from `plugin/`; focused tests for all touched modules pass.

documents.proposal:
# Proposal: Remediate slop defects (2026-06-25 scan)

## Why

A repository slop scan on 2026-06-25 surfaced 9 quality defects in `plugin/`. All 9 were independently re-validated against current source by direct file inspection — every one is real. The hallucination scan was clean. See per-finding evidence below.

## What Changes

Remediate or explicitly disposition each of the 9 findings (dispositions firmed by discovery — see Discovery Findings).

## User Outcomes

- Multi-session worktree ownership is safe on multi-user hosts (no false lease reclaim of live peers).
- Agenda data is durable — malformed lines are surfaced, never silently destroyed by compaction.
- Operational failures (archive cleanup, mesh issue creation) are observable instead of silently swallowed.
- The change-lifecycle code is easier to maintain (reduced duplication, single sources of truth).
- Every finding has a recorded disposition — fixed, documented non-actionable, or deferred-with-tracking.

## Affected Code

- `plugin/src/utils/worktree-lease.ts` (+ shared liveness helper under `utils/`)
- `plugin/src/storage/agenda.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/change.ts`
- `plugin/src/integrations/mesh-issues.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/trunk-write-firewall.ts` (docstring only — see disposition)
- `plugin/src/tools/gate.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/worktree/state.ts`, `plugin/src/temporal/visibility-claim-queries.ts`, `plugin/src/temporal/list-change-workflows.ts` (+ raw-literal sites in `archive-helpers/git-finalize.ts`, `worktree/census.ts`, `worktree/triage.ts`, `worktree/index.ts`)
- Co-located test files for each touched module.

## Related Repositories

Single-repo change (`advance`). No product/cross-repo scope.

## Constraints

- `pnpm run check` (from `plugin/`) must pass; touched-module focused tests must pass.
- Layer boundaries enforced by tests must be preserved (workflow-bundle import boundary, context-snapshot purity).
- Correctness fixes follow TDD (RED reproduction before fix) per P24.
- Structural correctness before heuristic (P33).
- No public schema drift without `pnpm run schemas:generate`.

## Scope

### In Scope

- Remediation or explicit documented disposition of all 9 validated findings.
- New/updated tests proving each correctness fix and guarding regressions.
- Touched-scope same-pattern cleanup (P23/P25) within the affected modules.

### Out of Scope

- Repo-wide complexity reduction beyond named findings (ROADMAP #82).
- Rewriting the Temporal recovery model beyond deduplicating existing glue (STRUCT-002).
- Hardening the firewall against shell indirection — spec-classified accepted residual risk (QUAL-001).
- Tooling-env scanner coverage gaps (eslint/knip root run, ast-grep, jscpd).

### Must Not

- Must not silently drop any finding.
- Must not weaken the trunk write-protection boundary.
- Must not break the exclusive-worktree-ownership invariant while fixing lease liveness.
- Must not introduce new silent-failure catches while fixing existing ones.

---

## Discovery Findings (2026-06-25)

### Current State (validated)

| ID | Status | Disposition (proposed) |
|---|---|---|
| QUAL-002 | real correctness bug | FIX — `worktree-lease.ts:123` `isPidAlive` returns `false` on EPERM; siblings `session/index.ts:47` + `worker-lock.ts:180` treat EPERM/non-ESRCH as alive. Converge on one shared helper. |
| QUAL-003 | real correctness bug | FIX — `agenda.ts:113` `parseLine` drops malformed lines silently; `project-wisdom.ts:203` + `reflection.ts:269` already log "Skipping malformed JSON line". Bring agenda to parity + guard compaction against parse-loss. |
| QUAL-004 | real silent failure | FIX — 4 `// warning-only` empty catches in `change.ts` (4240, 4252, 4267, **4605** — one more than the original 3). Emit real warnings. |
| QUAL-005 | real silent failure | FIX — `mesh-issues.ts:157` parse failure returns `exitCode: 0`. Add explicit `parseFailed` signal; callers treat as failure. |
| QUAL-001 | real but **spec-sanctioned** | DOCUMENT non-actionable — direct `rm`/`cp`/`mv`/`sed -i`/`tee`/redirects ARE handled; missing forms (find -exec, xargs, eval, bash -c, command substitution) are all shell indirection, which `advance-meta` spec explicitly classifies as "accepted residual risk." Optional: docstring cite. |
| STRUCT-002 | real duplication | FIX — `change.ts:1618-1677` triple-duplicated workflow-completed recovery glue. Centralize into one helper. |
| STRUCT-003 | real duplication | FIX — `gate.ts:130` vs `change.ts:1237` near-identical poll loops (60/500). Extract shared helper. |
| STRUCT-004 | real duplication | FIX — `adv/change/` defined in 3 modules + raw `change/` literals. Single exported prefix contract. |
| STRUCT-001 | real structural | DECISION — `change.ts` 5422-line god-object; overlaps deferred ROADMAP #82. Defer vs include (user decision). |

### Conflict Scan

- `adv_change_validate`: passed, only expected NO_TASKS/NO_DELTAS warnings (own-change pre-prep).
- `adv_change_list includeArchived`: timed out (archive scan, env-slow). Known related work: archived `fixSlopScanFindings` (2026-05-23) — different/older scan; its complexity deferral = ROADMAP #82 (overlaps STRUCT-001 only).
- `adv_agenda_list`: 284 pending items; `ag-VH206pZN` (extract worktree/index.ts modules) is thematically adjacent but a different file — no direct conflict.

### Related Pattern Scan (P25)

- isPidAlive: 3 implementations confirmed. `worktree-lease.ts:123` is the outlier (EPERM→false); `session/index.ts` + `worker-lock.ts` are conservative (EPERM→alive) and accept an injectable `isPidAlive`. → converge.
- Silent malformed-JSONL drop: `agenda.ts` is the outlier; `project-wisdom.ts:203` + `reflection.ts:269` log. → bring agenda to parity.
- `// warning-only` empty catches: 4 sites in `change.ts` (4240/4252/4267/4605). Other `// ignore` sites (snapshot-scan.ts, index.ts:971, worktree/triage.ts:409) are scanner/cleanup contexts — left as-is unless proven defects.

### Edge Cases

- QUAL-002: EPERM (cross-user PID) → must be alive; PID reuse after wrap → out of scope (pre-existing); ESRCH → dead.
- QUAL-003: malformed line mid-file; empty file; meta-only line; compaction triggered immediately after a bad append.
- QUAL-005: gh emits warning to stdout before JSON; empty stdout exit 0; valid JSON missing `html_url`.

### Open Design Questions

1. **STRUCT-001 inclusion** — Trust: joint (user). Blast radius: broad across `change.ts` consumers/tests. Alternatives: (a) defer to ROADMAP #82 [recommended], (b) include now. → ask user.
2. **QUAL-001 disposition** — Trust: joint (user). Blast radius: low. Alternatives: (a) document non-actionable per spec + optional docstring [recommended], (b) override spec and harden against indirection [out of scope unless user wants it]. → ask user.
3. **Spec-law capture** — Trust: joint (user). Should the lease-liveness + agenda-durability invariants become new `rq-*` spec law, or just be fixed with tests? → ask user.
4. **QUAL-002 helper shape** — Trust: agent. Resolved: extract shared `utils/` liveness helper, reuse in all 3 sites.
5. **QUAL-005 contract** — Trust: agent. Resolved: explicit `parseFailed` flag.

### Draft Spec Deltas (firmed in design; subject to "spec-law capture" decision)

- **rq-worktreeLeaseLiveness01** (`worktree-lifecycle`, optional): Given a lease held by another PID, When the liveness probe throws EPERM or a non-ESRCH error, Then the lease MUST be treated as alive and MUST NOT be reclaimed.
- **rq-agendaDurableParse01** (`advance-meta`, optional): Given an agenda JSONL file with a malformed line, When the agenda is loaded, Then the malformed line MUST be logged; And auto-compaction MUST NOT permanently discard unparsed content without surfacing it.
- All other findings: **No spec delta required** — QUAL-001 already covered (residual risk); QUAL-004/005 internal observability; STRUCT-002/003/004 behavior-preserving.

### Completeness Verification

- **Problem-completeness:** HIGH confidence. All 9 findings re-validated at source; P25 related-pattern scan extended QUAL-004 (3→4 sites) and confirmed agenda/lease as outliers vs existing good patterns. No sole-chokepoint claim relied upon.
- **Solution-scope:** HIGH confidence. Secondary surfaces (`index.ts:971`, `snapshot-scan.ts`, `worktree/triage.ts:409` ignore-catches) classified **out of scope** (scanner/cleanup contexts, not defects). No silent deferral.

### LBP Check

Internal refactor + bug-fix change; no external library/service viable. External-solution check: skipped (purely internal). Fixes align with existing in-repo good patterns (injectable liveness helper, malformed-line logging) — LBP = converge on the established internal patterns.

### AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS

B1  ADVISORY  Boundaries  STRUCT-001 inclusion is an open scope decision.
  Evidence: "DECISION — `change.ts` 5422-line god-object; overlaps deferred ROADMAP #82. Defer vs include (user decision)."
  Reason: surfaced for explicit user resolution in clarification round; not silently deferred.

Coverage: B:P F:C S:C M:C — no CRITICAL, no 2+ HIGH. Continue.
documents.problemStatement:
## Why

A repository slop scan (2026-06-25) surfaced 9 source-confirmed quality defects in `plugin/`. All 9 were independently validated against current source — each is real, not a false positive.

### Runtime correctness bugs (2)

- **QUAL-002** (`plugin/src/utils/worktree-lease.ts:123`): `isPidAlive` returns `false` on any `process.kill(pid, 0)` exception, including `EPERM`. Sibling implementations (`tools/session/index.ts:47`, `temporal/worker-lock.ts:180`) deliberately treat `EPERM`/non-`ESRCH` as alive. On a multi-user host, signal-0 against another user's PID yields `EPERM` → the live peer's lease is treated as dead → `reclaimStaleLease` overwrites it → two sessions believe they exclusively own the same worktree. Breaks the exclusive-ownership invariant.
- **QUAL-003** (`plugin/src/storage/agenda.ts:113`): `parseLine` catches JSON/schema failures and returns `null` with no logging; `loadAgenda` silently skips them; `maybeAutoCompactAgenda` then rewrites only the well-formed subset, permanently destroying malformed lines. Contradicts the module's append-only durability guarantee.

### Silent-failure handlers (2)

- **QUAL-004** (`plugin/src/tools/change.ts:4239`): three archive-cleanup catches labeled `// warning-only` emit no warning — no `logger`, no debug log. Cleanup failures are invisible until hygiene tools rediscover the leakage.
- **QUAL-005** (`plugin/src/integrations/mesh-issues.ts:157`): `JSON.parse` failure returns `exitCode: 0` (success) with empty payload, so callers see success-without-URL and never detect the parse failure.

### Security-boundary heuristic gap (1)

- **QUAL-001** (`plugin/src/tools/trunk-write-firewall.ts:279`): `classifyDestructiveBash` only recognizes destructive intent when `cp`/`mv`/`rm`/`sed -i`/`tee`/redirect is `tokens[0]`. It misses `find … -exec rm`, `xargs rm`, `eval`, `bash -c`, interpreter `-c`, command substitution, and background/subshell forms — a heuristic owning a write-protection boundary (P33).

### Structural / duplication issues (4)

- **STRUCT-001** (`plugin/src/tools/change.ts`): 5422-line module; `changeTools` is a single 3329-line object literal inlining 12 tool handlers. Overlaps with deferred follow-up ROADMAP #82.
- **STRUCT-002** (`plugin/src/tools/change.ts:1618-1677`): workflow-completed recovery glue duplicated verbatim across 3 consecutive catch blocks plus 9 dynamic `import()` sites.
- **STRUCT-003** (`gate.ts:130` vs `change.ts:1237`): two near-identical gate-completion polling loops (60 attempts / 500ms) with separate local delay wrappers.
- **STRUCT-004** (`worktree/state.ts:219-220` + 2 more): `change/` and `adv/change/` prefixes redefined across 3+ modules with raw-literal usage elsewhere.

## Desired Outcome

Each finding is either fixed with verification evidence, or explicitly deferred with documented rationale. No finding is silently dropped.

## Note

A prior change `fixSlopScanFindings` (archived 2026-05-23) addressed a different/older slop scan. This change is the remediation vehicle for the 2026-06-25 scan.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement

## Objectives

1. Fix the 4 runtime/silent-failure defects: lease liveness (QUAL-002), agenda durability (QUAL-003), archive-cleanup warnings (QUAL-004), mesh-issue parse signaling (QUAL-005).
2. Remove the 3 duplication defects via single sources of truth: recovery glue (STRUCT-002), gate polling (STRUCT-003), change/workflow prefixes (STRUCT-004).
3. Capture the two corrected correctness invariants as new spec law.
4. Record explicit dispositions for the 2 non-fix findings: QUAL-001 (residual risk) and STRUCT-001 (deferred to ROADMAP #82).

## Success Criteria

- **SC1** — Multi-session worktree ownership is safe: a live peer's lease is never reclaimed due to an inconclusive liveness probe.
- **SC2** — Agenda data is durable: malformed lines are observable and never silently destroyed by compaction.
- **SC3** — Archive-cleanup and mesh-issue operational failures are observable, not silently swallowed.
- **SC4** — Change-lifecycle code has single sources of truth for recovery glue, gate polling, and change/workflow prefixes.
- **SC5** — Every one of the 9 findings has a recorded disposition; none silently dropped.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **AC1** — Lease liveness treats `EPERM`/non-`ESRCH` as alive (dead only on `ESRCH`); a test proves a live peer's lease is not reclaimed on `EPERM`.
- **AC2** — Loading agenda JSONL with a malformed line emits an observable log for the dropped line, and compaction does not permanently discard unparsed content without surfacing it; test covers malformed-line load + compaction.
- **AC3** — Archive-cleanup failure paths in `change.ts` emit an observable warning (no empty `// warning-only` catch); verification shows a warning when cleanup throws.
- **AC4** — A mesh-issue JSON parse failure is distinguishable from a genuine exit-0 success and is treated as a failure by callers; test covers exit-0 + malformed stdout.
- **AC5** — Workflow-completed recovery glue in `change.ts` has a single source of truth used by the previously-duplicated catch sites; existing recovery/archive tests pass.
- **AC6** — Gate-completion polling has a single shared implementation used by both `gate.ts` and the `change.ts` release-gate path; existing gate/archive tests pass.
- **AC7** — `change/` and `adv/change/` prefixes are defined once and imported; duplicate module-local definitions removed; tests pass.
- **AC8** — New spec requirements `rq-worktreeLeaseLiveness01` (worktree-lifecycle) and `rq-agendaDurableParse01` (advance-meta) are added with Given/When/Then; implementation conforms.
- **AC9** — QUAL-001 disposition recorded as accepted residual risk; `trunk-write-firewall` docstring cites the spec decision; no firewall behavior change.
- **AC10** — STRUCT-001 disposition recorded as deferred to ROADMAP #82; no `change.ts` decomposition in this change.
- **AC11** — `pnpm run check` passes from `plugin/`; focused tests for all touched modules pass.

## Constraints

- **C1** — `pnpm run check` passes from `plugin/`.
- **C2** — Preserve enforced layer boundaries (workflow-bundle import boundary; context-snapshot purity).
- **C3** — Correctness fixes follow TDD (RED reproduction before fix).
- **C4** — No public schema drift without `pnpm run schemas:generate`.
- **C5** — Structural-correctness-before-heuristic (P33) preserved.

## Avoidances

- **DONT1** — Do not weaken the trunk write-protection boundary.
- **DONT2** — Do not break the exclusive-worktree-ownership invariant.
- **DONT3** — Do not introduce new silent-failure catches while fixing existing ones.
- **DONT4** — Do not harden the firewall against shell indirection (spec-classified residual risk).
- **DONT5** — Do not decompose `change.ts` (STRUCT-001 deferred to ROADMAP #82).

## Preview Applicability

`visual_surface: false` — all changes are backend/plugin TypeScript with no browser-visible or visual output. Rationale: touched modules are storage, tools, utils, integrations, and temporal internals.

## Decisions

### User Decisions

1. **STRUCT-001 scope** — Defer the `change.ts` god-object decomposition to ROADMAP #82. Why it matters: broad blast radius; keeps this change focused and shippable.
2. **QUAL-001 disposition** — Document as accepted residual risk + add docstring citation; do not harden against shell indirection. Why it matters: the `advance-meta` spec already classifies shell indirection as residual risk; hardening would require a spec change.
3. **Spec-law capture** — Capture the lease-liveness and agenda-durability invariants as new `rq-*` spec requirements. Why it matters: specs-as-laws; durable enforcement beyond regression tests.

### Agent Decisions (LBP)

1. **QUAL-002 helper shape** — Extract a single shared liveness helper into `utils/` and reuse it across `worktree-lease.ts`, `session/index.ts`, and `worker-lock.ts`. Rationale: eliminates the 3-way divergence at its root; the two canonical sites already accept an injectable `isPidAlive`.
2. **QUAL-005 contract** — Surface parse failure as an explicit failure signal distinct from exit-0 success (rather than a sentinel exit code). Rationale: clearer caller contract; least-surprising.
3. **QUAL-003 compaction guard** — Mirror the existing `project-wisdom.ts` / `reflection.ts` malformed-line logging, and guard auto-compaction so it does not destroy unparsed content without surfacing. Rationale: in-repo precedent, minimal new surface.

## Deferred Questions

None — all open questions resolved.

## Sign-Off

Criteria approved by user via Tier A inline reply `approve` at the Phase 4.5.1 acceptance-criteria checkpoint (2026-06-25).
documents.design:
# Design

## Architecture Overview

Eight in-place remediations plus two spec-law additions, grouped into four workstreams. No architectural restructuring — each fix converges divergent code onto an existing in-repo pattern or single source of truth. All changes are internal to `plugin/src`; no public schema, tool surface, or workflow-bundle boundary changes.

| Workstream | Findings | Strategy |
|---|---|---|
| A. Process liveness | QUAL-002 | Extract one shared liveness helper; converge 3 implementations |
| B. Durable / observable I/O | QUAL-003, QUAL-004, QUAL-005 | Mirror existing logging patterns; surface failures |
| C. SSOT dedup | STRUCT-002, STRUCT-003, STRUCT-004 | Extract single helpers / constants |
| D. Dispositions + spec law | QUAL-001, STRUCT-001, AC8 | Docstring + spec requirements |

## Key Decisions

### KD1 — Shared process-liveness helper (QUAL-002, AC1)
Create `plugin/src/utils/process-liveness.ts` exporting `isProcessAlive(pid: number): boolean` — the single canonical implementation: `process.kill(pid,0)` success → `true`; `ESRCH` → `false`; `EPERM`/any other code → `true` (alive). Matches the already-correct semantics in `tools/session/index.ts:47` and `temporal/worker-lock.ts:189`.
- `utils/worktree-lease.ts` replaces its buggy local `isPidAlive` (EPERM→false) with the shared helper — the actual bug fix (`reclaimStaleLease:227` no longer reclaims a live peer's lease on EPERM).
- `tools/session/index.ts` **re-exports** `isProcessAlive` under the existing `isPidAlive` name (preserves test import `session/index.test.ts:18`) — it must NOT remain a third independent copy (validator caution resolved).
- `temporal/worker-lock.ts` default fallback (`isLockOwnerAlive`) delegates to the shared helper, preserving its injectable-`isPidAlive` seam for tests.
- Rationale: eliminates the 3-way divergence at the root (P25).

### KD2 — Agenda durability (QUAL-003, AC2, rq-agendaDurableParse01)
- `storage/agenda.ts:parseLine` logs on parse/schema failure via `appendDebugLog("agenda", "Skipping malformed line: …")`, mirroring `project-wisdom.ts:203` and `reflection.ts:269`.
- `loadAgenda` records a count of skipped malformed lines.
- `maybeAutoCompactAgenda` / `compactAgenda` MUST NOT rewrite (which drops malformed lines permanently) when the load skipped any line — emit a warning and skip compaction so the corrupt line survives for manual inspection. Existing pre-compact backup remains.
- Rationale: append-only durability honored; corruption becomes observable + recoverable. No new silent failure (DONT3).

### KD3 — Observable archive-cleanup warnings (QUAL-004, AC3)
Replace the 4 empty `// warning-only` catches in `change.ts` (4240, 4252, 4267, 4605) with real `logger.warn(...)` (logger already in scope). Message includes change id, cleanup op, error text.

### KD4 — Mesh-issue parse-failure signal (QUAL-005, AC4)
Add explicit `parseFailed?: boolean` to `MeshIssueResult`. On `JSON.parse` failure in `createMeshIssue`/`listMeshIssues`/`getGhIssue`, set `parseFailed: true` (keep raw exitCode/stderr). Update consumer `archive-mesh.ts:71-73` to treat `result.parseFailed` as an error (the current branch only checks `exitCode !== 0 && !ghNotFound`, missing the exit-0+parse-fail case).

### KD5 — Centralized completed-workflow recovery (STRUCT-002, AC5)
Extract `withCompletedWorkflowRecovery<T>(fn, ctx)` into `tools/_recovery-writers.ts`. It runs `fn`, catches, classifies via `isWorkflowCompletedError`, delegates once to `recoverReleaseGateViaDiskProjection`. Replace the 3 byte-identical catch blocks at `change.ts:1618-1677` and consolidate the repeated dynamic `import()`. Behavior-preserving; existing recovery/archive tests guard.

### KD6 — Shared gate-completion polling (STRUCT-003, AC6)
Extract `waitForGateCompletion(handle, gateId, {attempts, delayMs})`. `gate.ts:waitForGateCompletionResult` and `change.ts:waitForArchiveReleaseGateCompletion` both call it; `"release"` becomes an argument; single delay wrapper; identical 60/500 values retained.

### KD7 — SSOT change/workflow prefixes (STRUCT-004, AC7)
Add `CHANGE_BRANCH_PREFIX = "change/"` and `CHANGE_WORKFLOW_PREFIX = "adv/change/"` to `temporal/contracts.ts` (already owns `ADVANCE_TEMPORAL_TASK_QUEUE_PREFIX`; workflow-safe string constants).
- **Primary dedup (AC7 core):** replace the 3 duplicate `CHANGE_WORKFLOW_PREFIX` defs — `tools/worktree/state.ts:220` (path confirmed; validator's storage/ lookup was wrong), `temporal/visibility-claim-queries.ts:25`, `temporal/list-change-workflows.ts:124` — and `CHANGE_BRANCH_PREFIX` (`state.ts:219`) with imports.
- **Campsite literals (replace where safe):** `tools/archive-helpers/git-finalize.ts`, `tools/worktree/census.ts`, `tools/worktree/triage.ts`, `tools/worktree/index.ts`. Interpolated workflow-id/branch builders `temporal/client.ts:61` (`adv/change/${projectId}/${changeId}`) and `temporal/activities.ts:616` (`change/${changeId}`) are additional candidates — convert to `` `${CHANGE_WORKFLOW_PREFIX}${projectId}/${changeId}` `` only if low-risk during apply; leave with rationale otherwise.

### KD8 — QUAL-001 disposition (AC9)
No firewall behavior change. Add a docstring note to `classifyDestructiveBash` in `trunk-write-firewall.ts` that **cites** the residual-risk decision recorded in `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` (per `advance-meta` `rq-twf01.7`, which names ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md as the doc locus) rather than restating policy: shell indirection (find -exec, xargs, eval, bash -c, command substitution) is accepted residual risk; direct rm/cp/mv/sed -i/tee/redirect are covered.

### KD9 — STRUCT-001 disposition (AC10)
No code change. Record in executive summary; remains tracked as ROADMAP #82.

## ADR Drafts

None — no decision meets the 3-criteria rubric (all are reversible, unsurprising, conformance-to-existing-pattern fixes).

## Implementation Strategy

1. **Spec law first** (AC8): add `rq-worktreeLeaseLiveness01` to `.adv/specs/worktree-lifecycle/spec.json` (14→15 reqs; no ID collision confirmed) + `rq-agendaDurableParse01` to `.adv/specs/advance-meta/spec.json`, with doc mirrors if the spec has them. `pnpm run schemas:check` unaffected (specs ≠ public Zod schemas).
2. **A** QUAL-002: new `utils/process-liveness.ts` + RED test (EPERM→alive reclaim) → converge 3 sites.
3. **B** QUAL-003 (RED: malformed-line load + compaction-guard), QUAL-004 (warning emitted), QUAL-005 (RED: exit-0 + malformed stdout) → fixes.
4. **C** STRUCT-002/003/004 behavior-preserving refactors; existing suites guard.
5. **D** QUAL-001 docstring + dispositions.
6. **Final**: `pnpm run check` from `plugin/` + focused suites for every touched module.

TDD: workstreams A and B carry RED reproductions; C is behavior-preserving; D is docs/spec.

## LBP Analysis

Every fix converges on an existing in-repo good pattern rather than inventing a new abstraction: injectable liveness (session/worker-lock), malformed-line logging (project-wisdom/reflection), SSOT constants (temporal/contracts). Long-term-best: reduces variance, makes the codebase self-consistent. No external library applicable (purely internal).

## Affected Components

- `plugin/src/utils/process-liveness.ts` (new) + `worktree-lease.ts`, `tools/session/index.ts`, `temporal/worker-lock.ts`
- `plugin/src/storage/agenda.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/change.ts` (warnings + recovery helper + poll helper + prefix imports)
- `plugin/src/integrations/mesh-issues.ts` + `plugin/src/archive/archive-mesh.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/gate.ts`
- `plugin/src/temporal/contracts.ts` + `visibility-claim-queries.ts` + `list-change-workflows.ts` (+ campsite: client.ts, activities.ts)
- `plugin/src/tools/worktree/{state,census,triage,index}.ts`, `plugin/src/tools/archive-helpers/git-finalize.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/trunk-write-firewall.ts` (docstring)
- `.adv/specs/worktree-lifecycle/spec.json`, `.adv/specs/advance-meta/spec.json`
- Co-located `*.test.ts` for each.

## Design Leverage Scout

Scout: skipped — bounded prescribed remediation. Eight individually-scoped fixes, each converging on an existing in-repo pattern or SSOT; opportunity surface (shortcuts/reuse/parallelism/simplification) ~zero beyond the convergence already adopted in KD1/KD2/KD5–KD7. No cross-cutting leverage to discover.

## Design-Derived Criteria

- **DDC1** — `utils/process-liveness.ts` must not import from `storage/` or `tools/` (it is consumed by tool + temporal layers); trivially satisfied (pure `process.kill` wrapper).
- **DDC2** — `temporal/contracts.ts` additions must stay plain string constants to preserve workflow-bundle import safety (no node:* / storage imports).

## Risks / Mitigations

- **R1: KD5 recovery refactor changes behavior subtly.** Mitigation: behavior-preserving extraction verified by `recovery-probe.test.ts`, `gate.test.ts`, `change.test.ts`, archive-phase9 suites.
- **R2: KD2 compaction-skip could let agenda grow if a malformed line persists.** Mitigation: acceptable — surfacing + warning is the intended durability tradeoff; manual fix restores compaction. Documented in rq-agendaDurableParse01.
- **R3: KD7 prefix import from `temporal/contracts.ts` in temporal/ query modules.** Mitigation: string constants are workflow-safe; `workflow-bundle-boundary.test.ts` guards regressions.
- **R4: session/index.ts re-export must not break existing `isPidAlive` test import.** Mitigation: keep the `isPidAlive` named export binding (re-export, not re-implement).

## Validation Result

**Validator (adv-researcher, independent): CAUTION** — VALIDATED with 2 minor cautions, no conflicts.

Confirmed correct: EPERM→alive is the fail-safe direction for lease reclaim (AC1); KD7 does not breach the workflow-bundle boundary (`workflow-bundle-boundary.test.ts:45-54` forbids only node:*/storage/tools imports + defineUpdate; plain string consts are safe); KD2 closes a real data-loss path with no new silent failure (DONT3 respected); residual-risk claim is real (`advance-meta` spec.json:1791 + rq-twf01.7); new rq IDs do not collide (worktree-lifecycle has no rq-worktreeLeaseLiveness01).

Cautions — all resolved in this design:
1. **KD7 3rd prefix site** — validator looked in `storage/worktree/`; the real path is `tools/worktree/state.ts:219-220` (confirmed by read). Extra interpolated literals (`client.ts:61`, `activities.ts:616`) folded into KD7 as apply-time campsite candidates.
2. **KD1 SSOT** — `process-liveness.ts` is the single canonical impl; `session/index.ts` re-exports (not a 3rd copy). Made explicit in KD1.
3. **KD8 docstring** — now cites `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` per rq-twf01.7 rather than restating policy.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

## Outcome
Remediated all 9 source-validated slop-scan findings in `plugin/` — 7 fixed with TDD/behavior-preserving refactors, 2 dispositioned (QUAL-001 accepted residual risk, STRUCT-001 deferred), plus 2 new spec requirements capturing the corrected correctness invariants. Two independent acceptance reviewers returned clean verdicts.

## Verdict
APPROVED

## What Was Built
1. **QUAL-002 (lease liveness):** new `utils/process-liveness.ts` `isProcessAlive` (ESRCH→dead; EPERM/other→alive, fail-safe); converged 3 divergent implementations (`worktree-lease.ts`, `session/index.ts` re-export, `worker-lock.ts` delegate). A live peer's lease is no longer reclaimed on EPERM.
2. **QUAL-003 (agenda durability):** `agenda.ts` parseLine now distinguishes skip/malformed/meta/item; malformed lines logged via `appendDebugLog`; auto-compaction skips + warns when a load dropped malformed lines, so corrupt content is never destroyed.
3. **QUAL-004 (silent cleanup):** 3 empty `// warning-only` archive-cleanup catches in `change.ts` now emit `logger.warn` with change id + op + error.
4. **QUAL-005 (mesh parse signal):** added `parseFailed` flag to mesh-issue results; `archive-mesh` consumer treats a parse failure as an error instead of silent exit-0 success.
5. **STRUCT-002:** centralized completed-workflow recovery glue into `recoverReleaseGateIfWorkflowCompleted`, replacing 3 byte-identical catch blocks.
6. **STRUCT-003:** extracted shared `waitForGateCompletion` in `_adapters.ts`; `gate.ts` and `change.ts` release path delegate.
7. **STRUCT-004:** single-source `CHANGE_BRANCH_PREFIX`/`CHANGE_WORKFLOW_PREFIX` in `temporal/contracts.ts`; removed 3 duplicate defs + migrated campsite literals.
8. **Spec law:** `rq-worktreeLeaseLiveness01` (worktree-lifecycle 1.6.0) + `rq-agendaDurableParse01` (advance-meta 1.19.0), with Given/When/Then + doc mirrors.
9. **Dispositions:** QUAL-001 documented as accepted residual risk (firewall docstring cites rq-twf01.7 + ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md; no behavior change); STRUCT-001 deferred to ROADMAP #82.

## What Was Verified
- Verdict: APPROVED with 0 blockers, 0 issues (6 minor nits/suggestions deferred to harden).
- Tests: every correctness fix has a RED→GREEN test that fails on revert (independently confirmed). `pnpm run check` PASS; full suite green except 42 proven-pre-existing failures (env STSL timeouts + stale version assertions in untouched subsystems).
- Preview URL: not_applicable — backend/plugin TypeScript only, no browser-visible or visual output (visual_surface: false).
- Contract matrix: 26/26 rows passing/respected, 0 failing.

## Remaining Concerns
None blocking. Deferred to `/adv-harden`: campsite consistency of remaining raw `change/${id}` construction literals in `git-finalize.ts`/`change.ts`; optional symmetric parseFailed tests for `listMeshIssues`/`getGhIssue` (no live consumers today).

# Acceptance

Reviewed at: 2026-06-25T16:31:47.333Z

## Contract Review Matrix

| ID | Kind | Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SC1 | success_criterion | **SC1** — Multi-session worktree ownership is safe: a live peer's lease is never reclaimed due to an inconclusive liveness probe. | pass | Multi-session worktree ownership safe: process-liveness.ts isProcessAlive (EPERM→alive) + worktree-lease.test EPERM-reclaim case asserts a live peer's lease is not reclaimed (status 'blocked'). runId tr_mqtmm1vw. |
| SC2 | success_criterion | **SC2** — Agenda data is durable: malformed lines are observable and never silently destroyed by compaction. | pass | Agenda durable: agenda.test proves malformed lines logged + compaction preserves malformed content (skipped). runId tr_mqtmro5b. |
| SC3 | success_criterion | **SC3** — Archive-cleanup and mesh-issue operational failures are observable, not silently swallowed. | pass | Operational failures observable: change.ts archive-cleanup logger.warn (3 sites) + mesh-issues parseFailed flag surfaced by archive-mesh consumer. |
| SC4 | success_criterion | **SC4** — Change-lifecycle code has single sources of truth for recovery glue, gate polling, and change/workflow prefixes. | pass | Single sources of truth: recoverReleaseGateIfWorkflowCompleted (change.ts), waitForGateCompletion (_adapters.ts), CHANGE_BRANCH_PREFIX/CHANGE_WORKFLOW_PREFIX (contracts.ts). |
| SC5 | success_criterion | **SC5** — Every one of the 9 findings has a recorded disposition; none silently dropped. | pass | All 9 findings dispositioned: 7 fixed, QUAL-001 documented residual risk, STRUCT-001 deferred ROADMAP #82. Touched-scope scan clean. |
| AC1 | acceptance_criterion | **AC1** — Lease liveness treats `EPERM`/non-`ESRCH` as alive (dead only on `ESRCH`); a test proves a live peer's lease is not reclaimed on `EPERM`. | pass | worktree-lease.test.ts EPERM mock → reclaim returns 'blocked'; process-liveness.test.ts ESRCH→false/EPERM→true. Independent reviewer confirmed fail-safe direction. runId tr_mqtmm1vw. |
| AC2 | acceptance_criterion | **AC2** — Loading agenda JSONL with a malformed line emits an observable log for the dropped line, and compaction does not permanently discard unparsed content without surfacing it; test covers malformed-line load + compaction. | pass | agenda.test.ts: malformed line logged + skippedMalformed counted; compaction preserves malformed content. runId tr_mqtmro5b. |
| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | **AC3** — Archive-cleanup failure paths in `change.ts` emit an observable warning (no empty `// warning-only` catch); verification shows a warning when cleanup throws. | pass | change.ts 3 empty // warning-only catches → logger.warn(change id + op + error). change.archive-phase9 suite green. runId tr_mqtn8rbr. |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | **AC4** — A mesh-issue JSON parse failure is distinguishable from a genuine exit-0 success and is treated as a failure by callers; test covers exit-0 + malformed stdout. | pass | mesh-issues.test exit-0+malformed→parseFailed:true; archive-mesh.test consumer pushes error. runId tr_mqtmw722. |
| AC5 | acceptance_criterion | **AC5** — Workflow-completed recovery glue in `change.ts` has a single source of truth used by the previously-duplicated catch sites; existing recovery/archive tests pass. | pass | recoverReleaseGateIfWorkflowCompleted SSOT; 3 catch sites delegate. Behavior-preserving: recovery/archive suites 97/97. runId tr_mqtmywu3. |
| AC6 | acceptance_criterion | **AC6** — Gate-completion polling has a single shared implementation used by both `gate.ts` and the `change.ts` release-gate path; existing gate/archive tests pass. | pass | _adapters.waitForGateCompletion shared; gate.ts + change.ts delegate; 3 direct unit tests. gate + _adapters suites green. runId tr_mqtn5liz/tr_mqtn6sni. |
| AC7 | acceptance_criterion | **AC7** — `change/` and `adv/change/` prefixes are defined once and imported; duplicate module-local definitions removed; tests pass. | pass | contracts.ts single prefix defs; 3 duplicate defs removed + campsite literals; rg confirms no remaining module-local def. boundary+visibility+git-finalize 130/130. runId tr_mqtndvqf. |
| AC8 | acceptance_criterion | **AC8** — New spec requirements `rq-worktreeLeaseLiveness01` (worktree-lifecycle) and `rq-agendaDurableParse01` (advance-meta) are added with Given/When/Then; implementation conforms. | pass | rq-worktreeLeaseLiveness01 (worktree-lifecycle 1.6.0) + rq-agendaDurableParse01 (advance-meta 1.19.0) with Given/When/Then + doc mirrors; impl conforms (AC1/AC2 tests). |

Full suite green except 42 proven-pre-existing failures. |

| DONT1 | avoidance | **DONT1** — Do not weaken the trunk write-protection boundary. | respected | trunk-write-firewall classifyDestructiveBash body unchanged (docstring-only diff). |
